steps.title: Set up the perimeter equation
steps.content: The perimeter of triangle ABC equals the sum of its three sides. With perimeter = 17 inches, AB = 4 inches, and AC = 7 inches, we write: $$AB + AC + BC = 17$$ Substituting the known values: $$4 + 7 + BC = 17$$

steps.title: Solve for BC
steps.content: Simplifying the equation: $$11 + BC = 17$$ Therefore: $$BC = 6$$ That matches choice B — B is correct.
